$comment: Season dossier pack S15, The Insurgent's Last Climb (Golden Circus). Season-scoped coverage keyed to the underdog's year: the whip-round garages, the thousand small names on the bodywork, the backstreet's final assault on the establishment's summit. Ids are stable rendezvous keys, never rename. Read-side only.

templates.headline: THE INSURGENT'S LAST CLIMB: the circus passes the hat
templates.deck: A thousand small names against the establishment's gold
templates.summary: Season fifteen opens as the underdogs' great year: garages funded by whip-rounds and faith, aimed squarely at the summit.

sections.lead: {pool:dateline} Somewhere down the pit lane this winter, a garage sold everything not bolted to its car and lettered a thousand small names where the sponsors ought to be. That is the spirit of the season they are calling the insurgent's last climb: the backstreet's final, total assault on the establishment's gold.
sections.context: The grandees arrived with polished budgets and heritage. The believers arrived with collection tins and tools they own outright. The big board, blessedly, cannot tell the difference. {pool:swLadder}
sections.close: Sixteen rounds of climbing ahead. The summit does not care how anybody got up.

templates.headline: {subject} wins[[?venue: at {venue}]], and every small name on the car wins too
templates.deck: The collection tin beats the chequebook

sections.lead: {pool:dateline} Read the names on the flanks of the winning car tonight, because every one of them owns a piece of this. {subject} took the flag[[?venue: at {venue}]] for the people the establishment never sees, and the grandstand read the roll of honour aloud. {pool:swCrowd}

templates.headline: {subject} on the podium[[?venue: at {venue}]], chin up at the aristocracy
templates.deck: A rung the backstreet paid for

sections.lead: {pool:dateline} The insurgent's year measures itself in afternoons like this: {subject} on the podium, helmet off, looking down at garages that spent more on hospitality than some teams spent on everything. {pool:swCrowd}

templates.headline: POLE for {subject}[[?venue: at {venue}]], a front row nobody booked

sections.lead: {pool:dateline} The front row of a golden-age grid is supposed to be reserved seating, bought with budget and pedigree. {subject} took it anyway[[?venue: at {venue}]], with a lap paid for in nerve and a garage's whole winter. {pool:swBoard}
sections.close: The grandees will want the seat back tomorrow. They are welcome to try.

templates.headline: {subject} leads the insurgent's season: the climb reaches the ridge
templates.deck: A backstreet name on top of the establishment's board
templates.summary: {subject} took the championship lead in season fifteen.

sections.lead: {pool:dateline} The ridge is in sight, and the name on top of the big board belongs to the climb: {subject} leads the championship. Somewhere tonight a garage that mortgaged its future is reading the standings by lamplight and saying nothing at all. {pool:swBoard}
sections.close: The last stretch of any climb is the steepest. The summit has been warned.

templates.headline: The gamble shows its price: {subject} parks it[[?venue: at {venue}]]
templates.deck: A garage with no spare anything loses a day

sections.lead: {pool:dateline} When a garage bets everything on one car, the car carries everything, and today something underneath {subject} stopped carrying[[?venue: at {venue}]]. No spare programme, no second string, just a quiet machine and a long evening. {pool:wrenchNote}
sections.context: The climb does not end at one broken afternoon; it merely gets more expensive. {pool:swLadder}
sections.close: The crew will mend it with what they have. What they have has been enough so far.

templates.headline: {subject} in the wall[[?venue: at {venue}]], the reach exceeds the grip
templates.deck: The climb's oldest danger claims one

sections.lead: {pool:dateline} On a climb like this, the temptation is always to carry one corner more courage than the car can cash. {subject} carried it[[?venue: at {venue}]], and the wall banked the difference. The driver climbed out with the season's resolve intact and the car considerably less so. {pool:swCrowd}
sections.close: The believers will pass the hat once more. It has never stayed empty yet.
